goal: Persist runtime and DB diagnostics in completed Real Backend Test metrics so future performance work can be attributed correctly.
changed: Added LoadTestDiagnosticsSampler shared diagnostics capture service
Refactored LoadTestDiagnosticsEndpoint to use the shared sampler
Added diagnostics to RealLoadTestMetricPoint and RealLoadTestMetricsResponse
Persisted diagnostics from fake and k6 Real Backend runners
Updated dashboard snapshot parsing to expose persisted diagnostics
Updated dashboard to use persisted diagnostics when live samples are absent
Added backend and frontend tests for persisted diagnostics
intentionallyNotChanged: No public API query or DTO behavior change
No claim that this directly improves p95 or throughput

validations.result: passed: Contract 1 skipped, Component 122, Unit 56, Architecture 35, Integration 223
passed with 0 errors and 6 pre-existing warnings

validations.command: runtime Real Backend k6 smoke 5rps 5s maxVUs 20

expectedBackendTestImpact: Completed Real Backend Test can retain DB command/open latency samples
Dashboard can distinguish DB pressure from app/runtime pressure after a run completes
Future p95/throughput optimizations can be compared against DB, app, ThreadPool, and GC movement
risks: k6 currently persists a completion snapshot rather than a high-resolution timeline
DB command sample count depends on diagnostics interceptor registration
This is attribution work and does not directly improve request throughput
recommendation: Use persisted diagnostics in the next spike comparison, then choose DB/pool/query, payload/serialization, or runtime allocation work based on which metric is actually red.
